_comment: Health-review backlog — the parking lot for MINOR SYSTEM / PIPELINE / DOC issues a session noticed but did not fix (a stale comment, a small wiring gap, a doc that needs a touch, a flaky test). Per docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md the session-end routine appends here instead of letting small issues rot. The autonomous /health-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, fixes what it can, and marks it resolved. Do NOT use this for live-trading incidents or anything Tier-2/3 that needs immediate attention — raise those with the operator in chat. Three-way split (2026-05-26): STRATEGY / TRADING follow-ups go in docs/claude/performance-review-backlog.json; AI/ML EXPERIMENT follow-ups go in docs/claude/ml-review-backlog.json. Item ids here: BL-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.source: /health-review 2026-06-03 — root-causing a graceful ict-trader-live restart at 04:33:33Z (uptime 5954s at the 06:14Z pull).
items.title: Nightly IBKR-reset: the 3 new MES/MGC/MHG futures strategies block the main-thread tick loop on IB socket timeouts long enough to stall the heartbeat and trip the liveness-watchdog autoheal (whole pipeline restarts, incl. live BTCUSDT)
items.description: VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA 2026-06-03. During IBKR's overnight server-reset (~04:27Z) the IB Gateway session dropped (ict-trader-live journal: a flood of `Error 1100 Connectivity between IBKR and Trader Workstation has been lost` from 04:27:47, then usfarm/ushmds/secdefnj farms broke). The new daily futures strategies (mes_trend_long_1d / mgc_pullback_1d / mhg_pullback_1d, added 2026-06-02 in S-MEANTIME-WSA) each call IBMarketData.get_ohlcv on the MAIN-THREAD pipeline loop; with the gateway down each fetch blocks on a ~10s connect TimeoutError, and three of them per tick cycle (on top of BTCUSDT) pushed the per-tick wall time past the heartbeat-staleness threshold. Result: ict-liveness-watchdog journal (#2663) shows `[CRITICAL] Trader heartbeat stale` at 04:31:31 (last beat 5m), then at 04:33:34 `[ACTION] Autoheal dispatched: systemctl restart ict-trader-live.service. Trigger: heartbeat stale 7m, 3 consecutive checks. systemctl exit=0`. The trader restarted cleanly (Started 04:33:34, validation passed 04:33:50) and is healthy now (uptime ~100min at review, all 11 strategies eval'ing). So the autoheal worked AS DESIGNED — but a NIGHTLY ~7-min whole-pipeline stall + restart (which also interrupts live BTCUSDT order/monitor and fires a [CRITICAL] Telegram every reset) is an unwanted side-effect of the futures expansion. Pre-expansion, an IB market-data gap did not stall the heartbeat. Distinct from (but co-fired with) the IB-gateway-watchdog auto-heal (BL-20260528-001, which restarts the gateway container, not the trader) and surfaces BL-20260525-003 again (the IB 'no candle data' raises are swallowed as bare warnings, invisible on the diag audit surface). The liveness-watchdog boot-grace (--boot-grace-seconds 600) only covers a HOST boot, not this stall.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever ict-trader-live shows a graceful systemd restart in the ~04:25-04:45Z IBKR-reset window with a liveness-watchdog `[ACTION] Autoheal dispatched` preceding it. Re-pull journalctl?unit=ict-liveness-watchdog.service and ?unit=ict-trader-live.service for the overnight window.
items.resolution_criteria: The MES/MGC/MHG IB market-data fetch no longer stalls the main-thread heartbeat through an IBKR reset — e.g. (a) a tight per-fetch IB connect/read timeout (sub-second / 1-2s, not ~10s) so a wedged gateway can't accumulate enough blocking to cross the staleness threshold; and/or (b) move the IB candle fetch off the main thread / cache last-good candles across a transient gateway outage so the tick completes and the heartbeat keeps firing; and/or (c) have the liveness-watchdog suppress autoheal during the known nightly IBKR-reset window the same way boot-grace does. Verify across >=1 overnight reset that the trader does NOT restart and the heartbeat stays fresh while the IB-gateway-watchdog heals the gateway. Tier-2 (touches src/main.py tick loop / src/exchange/ib_connector / the watchdog) — operator-approved PR; not editable from /health-review.

items.source: /health-review — operator-flagged squeeze_breakout_4h Telegram sequence (2026-05-31 16:00-16:15 UTC)
items.title: Misleading 'TRADE OPENED — SHORT' ping on an intent_reduce leg (carries parent LONG's entry/SL/TP)
items.description: VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA, NOT A REAL TRADING BUG — notification-only. When the multi-strategy intent layer emits an intent_reduce/close leg, coordinator._delta_to_legs does dataclasses.replace(pkg, direction=delta.side) to flip the leg's direction to the opposite (reduce-only) side, but KEEPS the parent package's entry/sl/tp. execute_pkg then fires enqueue_trade_open(side=pkg.direction) (execute.py:881) for the reduce leg, so the operator sees '🟢 TRADE OPENED — BTCUSDT SHORT' with Entry 73693.8 / SL 72385.78 (BELOW) / TP 80989.49 (ABOVE) — SL/TP levels that are correct for a LONG, not a short. Ground truth (trade 1949, bybit_1): setup_type=intent_reduce, a 0.007 reduce-only SHORT leg trimming the open LONG position (pkg-838fe5ef, dir=long, the squeeze 4h winner), NOT a new short entry. The position was never short. Two sub-issues: (a) a reduce/close leg should not fire the 'TRADE OPENED' lifecycle ping at all (it's a partial close, not an open) — or should be labelled 'POSITION REDUCED'; (b) if it does ping, it must not show the parent's open-side SL/TP on the opposite-side leg. Fix is in the notification path only (execution_diagnostics.enqueue_trade_open call site in execute.py + the reduce-leg branch in coordinator), Tier-2 (touches execute.py / coordinator.py which the live VM consumes) — propose via PR, do not self-merge.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or operator confusion about reduce-leg pings.
items.resolution_criteria: An intent_reduce/close leg no longer emits a 'TRADE OPENED — <opposite side>' ping with the parent's open-side SL/TP. Either it emits a distinct 'POSITION REDUCED' notification, or it suppresses the open-ping for reduce_only legs. Verified against a live reduce leg.
items.resolution: Fixed by PR #2476 (merged Tier-2, 2026-05-31, per S-PROFIT-GAPS-2026-05-31 sprint log): 'reduce-only legs no longer fire the TRADE OPENED — SHORT ping with the parent long's SL/TP' (11 new tests). Fix is live on the running sha f91aa32 (post-#2476). Live re-verification against a fresh reduce leg is opportunistic — no reduce leg fired in this review's quiet window — but the notification-path code fix is shipped + tested. Closing on the fix; re-open only if a misleading reduce-leg ping recurs.

items.source: /health-review — operator-flagged squeeze_breakout_4h Telegram sequence (2026-05-31 16:00-16:15 UTC)
items.title: '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' fires on an expected flip_suppressed_hold_policy no-op (false alarm)
items.description: VERIFIED ON LIVE DATA, NOT A REAL TRADING BUG — alerting false-positive. coordinator.multi_account_execute emits the high-priority 'ALL accounts failed to dispatch' roll-up whenever NO account placed a trade in a round (line 1617: `not any(r.trade_id)`). But under FLIP_POLICY=hold (the live default since PR #2451), an opposite-side signal against an open position is INTENTIONALLY suppressed to a noop (intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy) — zero trades placed is the CORRECT, designed outcome, not a dispatch failure. On 2026-05-31 16:15 the squeeze short signal (id 1951 bybit_1 / 1952 bybit_2) was correctly held (open long present), yet the operator got a 🚨 ALL-accounts-failed alert. The roll-up should treat a round where every account result is an intent_noop (at_target / flip_suppressed_hold_policy / hold) as a benign no-op, NOT a failure — only genuine errors (RiskBreach, exchange_rejected, missing creds) should trip the 🚨 alarm. Fix in coordinator.py:1617 guard (exclude intent_noop reasons) + possibly execution_diagnostics.enqueue_all_accounts_failed_dispatch. Tier-2 (live-path file) — propose via PR.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or operator alarm-fatigue on flip-suppressed signals.
items.resolution_criteria: A dispatch round in which every account result is an intent_noop (at_target / flip_suppressed_hold_policy / flip-hold) no longer triggers the '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' high-priority ping; genuine all-fail rounds (risk/exchange/creds errors) still do. Verified against a flip-suppressed round.
items.resolution: Fixed by PR #2476 (merged Tier-2, 2026-05-31, per S-PROFIT-GAPS-2026-05-31): the '🚨 ALL accounts failed to dispatch' roll-up 'no longer fires when every account is a benign intent_noop (flip-suppressed/at-target).' LIVE-CONFIRMED this review: the trades pull (issue #2594) shows 30 consecutive vwap rows status=rejected reason=intent_noop:flip_suppressed_hold_policy across bybit_1+bybit_2 every ~8 min (2026-06-01 14:34→16:32Z) — exactly the trigger condition — and no '🚨 ALL accounts failed' alert accompanies them (the suppression is benign + silent as designed). Fix shipped + behaviour verified against live flip-suppressed rounds. Closing.

items.source: live verification during the trader-bot rebuild session (2026-05-25)
items.title: ib_paper account has no ib_port — MES order client cannot reach IB Gateway
items.description: The live trader logs `ib_client_for(ib_paper): no ib_port set (config or IB_PORT env) — cannot reach IB Gateway.` on every tick that routes to ib_paper. IB *market data* works (the gateway is up after the 2026-05-25 restart and MES candle fetches succeed via IBMarketData on 127.0.0.1:4002), so MES ticks complete fast and the heartbeat is stable. But the ib_paper account's ORDER client (src/units/accounts/clients.py::ib_client_for) has no ib_port configured, so it cannot place orders on MES — the account effectively collects signals/data but can't execute. If MES is intended to trade (paper), config/accounts.yaml::ib_paper needs `ib_port: 4002` (or the IB_PORT env on the trader unit). Pre-existing (predates this session); surfaced during post-deploy verification. Operator was informed in chat 2026-05-25 and chose to log it rather than fix immediately.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; surface to the operator (do NOT auto-edit accounts.yaml).
items.resolution_criteria: Operator decides whether MES should place live (paper) orders. If yes, set ib_port (4002) on the ib_paper account in config/accounts.yaml (Tier-3 change, operator-approved) and confirm the trader log no longer emits 'no ib_port set' + a smoke order reaches IB Gateway. If MES is data-only by design, document that and close.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: still open. Status snapshot shows ib_paper live=true on sha 6a7aef4; no journalctl pull this review (relay queue wedged after 13 min — 3 of 6 issues never returned). The order-client gap (separate from IB market-data which works) would still emit the per-tick warning. Remains operator-gated (Tier-3); no change.
items.resolution: PR #2167 (squashed as 288d9bf) forwarded ib_host/ib_port/ib_account/ib_client_id through _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile in src/runtime/order_monitor.py. The original diagnosis attributed the warning to the ORDER client (coordinator.py::multi_account_execute calling ib_client_for(account_cfg)), but that path's account_cfg already carries ib_port via getattr(account, 'ib_port', None) at coordinator.py:1045 — confirmed by reading the code. The actual emitter was the MONITOR RECONCILER path: _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile() built an account_cfg dict that only forwarded a subset of fields and dropped the IB connection keys, so account_open_positions(cfg) → ib_read_client_for(account) → ib_client_for(...) → account.get('ib_port') = None → warning + return None. Fired from 4 call sites in order_monitor.py (1523, 1877, 2491, 3463), each on a 60s monitor tick — hence the per-tick spam. Post-deploy verification on diag-request #2169 confirmed: 'ib_client_for warning count: 0' across 300 lines / ~5 ticks; reconciler successfully opened the IB read socket (clientId 9731 visible at 17:57:38). MES order dispatch was never actually blocked — the data-collection-only framing in the original description was incorrect.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-25 — root-causing the fade_breakout_4h silence
items.title: intent_multiplexer swallows a raising strategy builder with only logger.warning — no audit/diag event, so a broken strategy is invisible in the diag surface
items.description: src/runtime/intent_multiplexer.py::_collect_intents (the live path; pipeline uses multiplexed_intents) catches a builder exception with only `logger.warning('strategy X raised ... skipping this tick')` and continues. Unlike the LEGACY multiplexer (src/runtime/pipeline.py::multiplexed_signal_builder, ~L243-252) which emits a `report('strategy_builder','exception', strategy=...)` audit event, the intent layer emits NOTHING to signal_audit.jsonl / the diag relays. Consequence surfaced this review: fade_breakout_4h has been raising 'No numeric types to aggregate' every tick (origin: the ATR/ADX pd.concat([...],axis=1).max(axis=1) in src/units/strategies/fade_breakout_4h.py around L104/118) and producing ZERO eval events / zero shadow data — but because the catch is silent, there was no `*_eval`, no exception event, and no diag trace; it was only visible in journalctl. A failing strategy should be observable from the read-only diag surface, not just the systemd journal.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or when a strategy is mysteriously silent in the audit tail.
items.resolution_criteria: intent_multiplexer._collect_intents emits a report()/audit event (e.g. 'strategy_builder'/'exception' with strategy + reason) on a builder exception, mirroring the legacy multiplexer, so a raising strategy shows up in signal_audit.jsonl and /api/diag/audit. (Code change — Tier-1 observability; not editable from /health-review which only writes docs + this backlog.) NOTE: this is the OBSERVABILITY gap only; the fade_breakout_4h crash itself is a separate Tier-3 strategy bug surfaced to the operator in the 2026-05-25 health-review response.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: observability gap OBSERVED AGAIN. journalctl 04:50-04:54 shows the IB/MES outage making every MES strategy raise 'no candle data returned for MES — skipping this tick' as a bare logger.WARNING in intent_multiplexer; those MES skips emit NO *_eval and NO audit event, so MES strategy silence is invisible on the diag surface (only journalctl). Exactly the swallow this item flags. Remains operator-gated (Tier-1 code change, not editable from /health-review).

items.source: 2026-05-25 per-model status review (operator asked to fix the two degenerate shadow models)
items.title: MES regime models (mes-regime-5m/15m) collapse to one class (f1_volatile=0) — model/feature limitation, NOT data-blocked
items.description: mes-regime-5m-baseline-v1 + mes-regime-15m-baseline-v1 never predict 'volatile' (recall_volatile=precision_volatile=f1_volatile=0; acc ~0.49-0.53). IMPORTANTLY this is NOT the missing-MES-trade-data blocker: these train on MES market_features (price history), which IS present and BALANCED — 5m: 60957 range / 25916 volatile (~70/30); 15m: 17694/11278 (~61/39). So it will NOT self-resolve when IBKR is approved + MES starts trading. Root cause: RegimeClassifierTrainer is a per-bucket MODAL-class classifier (ml/trainers/regime_classifier.py) keyed on the backward-looking vol_bucket; on MES that backward vol_bucket does not separate the (forward-vol-based) regime_label, so every bucket is range-majority and argmax always picks 'range'. BTC works because BTC vol is autocorrelated; MES intraday isn't. Operator decision 2026-05-25: LEAVE manifests as-is for now (do NOT demote to research_only); revisit in a dedicated model-training session.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit when a model-training session is scheduled, or once richer/live MES history is available.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-regime-{5m,15m} eval shows f1_volatile > 0 with a non-trivial macro_f1, via a feature/model change (e.g. more vol buckets, probability-threshold eval instead of argmax, a stronger/continuous vol feature, or a forward-vol-aware feature). Trainer-side (autonomous) work; verify on the trainer VM before/after. Distinct from the MES *trade*-data models (mes-execution-quality / mes-setup-quality / mes-trade-outcome-winrate) which ARE empty_dataset-blocked and self-resolve once MES trades.

items.title: Stale Cloudflare Pages integration fails CI on every ict-trader-dashboard commit (vestigial 'ict-trader-widget' project)
items.description: The ict-trader-dashboard repo runs a 'Cloudflare Pages' check that FAILS in ~0s on every commit/PR (seen on PR #71, commits across the session). items.source: operator flagged a live BTCUSDT trade whose TP (~143,210) sat ~2x the BTC price / above the all-time high
items.title: EXPECTED, NOT A BUG: trend_donchian (+ fade/squeeze) place a far ~50R 'unreachable sentinel' TP — don't re-flag it as a broken take-profit
items.description: A live trend_donchian BTCUSDT long showed TP=143,210.10 with SL=76,355.40 (entry ~77,666; current ~77,300). This is the DESIGNED 50R sentinel, not a malfunction. trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h have NO fixed profit target (matches their backtests, which have no TP) — the Chandelier ATR trailing stop is the sole profit exit. But the pipeline's dispatch gate requires every order to carry BOTH an SL and a TP, so the TP is parked tp_r*risk away (tp_r=50.0 in config/strategies.yaml:153/216/259) where it can't realistically fill (max single-trade excursion in 3yr backtest ~11R). Signature to recognize it: reward:risk ~= 50:1. Math check on this trade: TP = entry + 50*(entry - SL) => 77,666.27 + 50*1,310.87 = 143,209.8 ~= 143,210.10. Formula lives at src/units/strategies/trend_donchian.py:190/195 (order_package) and the trade is protected by the live ratcheting trail in monitor() at trend_donchian.py:285 (moves SL up only, never loosens). The TP-cross close at trend_donchian.py:342 exists only for completeness. A far TP carries no margin/liquidation risk; if BTC ever printed 143k the trade would simply close at +50R. The only live strategy that emits this is trend_donchian (turtle_soup uses tp1_at_r 1.0 / tp2_at_r 3.0, ict_scalp tp_at_r 1.5, vwap a VWAP-anchored TP) — fade/squeeze share the 50R sentinel and (as of 2026-05-25) also run execution: live, so their 50R TPs now appear on real bybit_2 order packages too — still the intended sentinel (see BL-20260525-008).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that scores a trend_donchian / fade_breakout_4h / squeeze_breakout_4h order package and sees a TP far from price (~50R / ~2x BTC). Recognize it as the intended sentinel — do NOT score it as a TP defect.
items.resolution_criteria: Informational standing note; nothing to fix. Close only if the 50R-sentinel mechanism is itself removed (e.g. the pipeline stops requiring a TP for trail-only strategies, or tp_r is changed in an operator-approved Tier-3 PR).

items.source: Investigating the trend_donchian stuck-watchdog alert (trade 1722 / pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339); fixed the alert symptom in PR #2002
items.title: Zero-sized same-direction reinforcement signals persist as open order_packages (clutter + watchdog churn)
items.description: trend_donchian (and any breakout strategy) re-fires a same-direction signal on each new bar while the breakout persists. The intent multiplexer correctly refuses to pyramid onto an already-open position — it sizes the reinforcement to target_qty=0.0 (aggregation_reason 'same_direction_reinforcement: ... max target_qty=0.0', sized_qty_by_account={}) — but the zero-qty package is STILL persisted as status='open' with linked_trade_id set, even though no exchange order was placed. These ghost packages never close on their own; their updated_at goes stale and they accumulate in order_packages, and they are what trips the stuck-strategy watchdog (the live snapshot for trade 1722 showed pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339 and earlier ones already watchdog_released_alive). PR #2002 made the watchdog timeframe-aware so it no longer false-alerts on these, but the underlying clutter remains. Root fix: a reinforcement that sizes to 0 (no order to monitor) should NOT create a lingering open order_packages row — either skip persisting it, persist it with a terminal status (e.g. status='skipped'/'no_order' + close_reason), or auto-close it so it's never gated/monitored/watchdog'd. Verify the strategy_monocle gate (pipeline.py::_has_open_package_for_strategy) and the per-strategy gates still behave correctly afterward.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever order_packages shows multiple open same-direction zero-qty packages for one live position. Investigate the intent_multiplexer / order-package persistence path.
items.resolution_criteria: A same-direction reinforcement that aggregates to target_qty=0 no longer leaves a lingering status='open' order_packages row (it is skipped or persisted terminal), so ghost packages stop accumulating and the stuck-watchdog has nothing benign to flag. Order-path change (Tier-2): open a draft PR + operator review.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: STILL HAPPENING — now 19/19 in-window packages (2026-05-28 04:04-06:04) carry aggregated_target_qty=0.0 + empty sized_qty_by_account, linked_trade_id=null, and ALL ended status=orphaned by the 5min reconciler (100%). Both edge cases present: same_direction_reinforcement AND conflict_resolved_by_priority (vwap priority=40 long beats trend_donchian short, then aggregates to 0). journalctl 06:00 confirms the downstream effect: strategy_monocle skips dispatch ('strategy=vwap already has open package pkg-5d6aa3051b364cdc') because the prior ghost is still open — self-perpetuating churn until the 5min orphan. Code change still pending — Tier-2.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-27 — three of six diag-relay issues opened back-to-back never returned a comment
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay: subset of issues opened in quick succession never comment+close (stuck OPEN; 38 stale opens accumulated since 2026-05-17)
items.description: This review opened six diag issues at 2026-05-27T07:19:28..54Z. Three returned comments within 30s and auto-closed normally (#2125 status, #2128 order_packages, #2130 trainer-vm). Three never returned a comment in 16+ min and stayed OPEN (#2126 services, #2127 audit, #2129 trades). Cross-check: there are now 38 open vm-diag-request issues going back to #1327 (2026-05-17) — a chronic accumulation. Hypothesis: when issues open within seconds of each other, the concurrency-group serializer (`group: vm-diag-snapshot, cancel-in-progress: false`) starts them but a subset of runs fail/timeout SSH or curl in a way that the post-step `if: cancelled()` / `if: failure()` comment-back step doesn't fire — so the issue is left open with no comment. The 2026-05-16 commentary in vm-diag-snapshot.yml already flagged this exact failure mode (#1278/#1281/#1284). Effect: /health-review can't reliably pull a full state set in one fire; chronic open-issue clutter on the repo. Code-level fix needed in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml: add an always() post-step that drops a 'workflow ran but produced no output / failed at step X' comment so the issue stops looking 'in flight'.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or any session that opens >2 diag issues in quick succession and notices subsequent ones never return.
items.resolution_criteria: vm-diag-snapshot.yml carries an `if: always()` (or `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()`) closing-comment step that ALWAYS posts a result-or-error comment + closes the issue, so no diag issue can be left silently OPEN. Then sweep the 38 stale open issues — close as 'workflow run silently failed; re-open if you need the data'. Code change (Tier-1 observability); not editable from /health-review.
items.resolution: ROOT-CAUSE refined 2026-05-27: vm-diag-snapshot.yml ALREADY carries `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()` + an implicit-success handler that together cover every terminal job state. The remaining gap is when GitHub's webhook layer batches rapidly-filed `issues.opened` events and silently drops some — the workflow never starts, so none of the handlers fire. There's no in-workflow fix for that. Instead, a SEPARATE sweeper workflow `.github/workflows/diag-relay-sweep.yml` was shipped (PR #2144, merged 2026-05-27 ~09:50Z): daily cron at 06:00 UTC + on-demand workflow_dispatch; finds open `vm-diag-request` / `trainer-vm-diag-request` issues >30 min old with no `github-actions[bot]` reply, comments + closes them. VERIFIED 2026-05-28: the first 06:00 UTC cron ran and cleared the entire stale backlog — `list_issues(label=vm-diag-request, state=OPEN)` returned 0 (the ~38 issues going back to #1327 are all closed). The webhook-drop wedge still recurs (this review's #2173/#2174/#2175 never returned and were closed manually), but that is now an accepted condition: the sweeper cleans up any silently-dropped issue within ≤24h, so no diag-request stays OPEN indefinitely. Mitigation confirmed working end-to-end.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: sweeper verified — 0 open vm-diag-request issues remain after the 06:00 cron. Re-wedge of 3 rapid-fired pulls observed but auto-cleanup confirmed. Re-fired one-at-a-time spaced ~70s apart (#2591/2592/2593/2594) — all four then returned cleanly. Confirms the accepted-condition framing: prevention isn't fixed, but spaced single fires work and the diag-relay-sweep cron will close the 4 stale opens within ≤24h. Operational note for future reviews: fire diag pulls SEQUENTIALLY, not in a burst. Staying resolved (mitigation holds).

items.source: session wrap-up — `python -m ml compare setup-quality-baseline-v0 setup-quality-lgbm-v2` after PR #2053 + #2067 cycles.
items.title: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 underperforms v0 baseline on n=76 closed trades — too little data for LightGBM
items.description: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 trained cleanly (PR #2053's LightGBM stack works end-to-end) but loses to setup-quality-baseline-v0 on the eval split: MAE 0.0710 vs 0.0478, MSE 0.0114 vs 0.0092 (n_eval=76). items.source: Today's IB Gateway wedge — Up 21h, upstream IB session dead.
items.title: IBC nightly auto-restart doesn't reliably recover the upstream IB session
items.description: IBC's `Auto restart time` is configured to 11:59 PM on the ib-gateway container (verified in the IBC log on issue #2165 post-recovery: `Auto restart time already set to 11:59 PM`). Yet today the container was 'Up 21 hours' with its upstream IB session entirely dead (Warning 2103 usfarm / 2105 ushmds / 2157 secdefnj all 'broken'; every API call timed out at 10s; 200/200 log lines were `remove Client <id>` with no upstream activity). Either IBC's nightly restart didn't fire, or it fired and didn't successfully re-authenticate to IB's data farms. The autonomous `vm-ib-gateway-recover` workflow is the current mitigation (Claude can dispatch it whenever the symptom recurs), but the root cause needs investigating. Candidates: 2FA timing on the nightly relogin, stale Java/Gateway state on Auto-restart that needs a hard container restart, or IB-side throttling on rapid reconnects. Probable scope: inspect IBC config (jts.ini, ibc.ini) + the docker entrypoint + gateway startup logs around the 11:59 PM window over multiple days to see if the restart fires at all.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; investigate via gateway-logs + provision_ib_gateway.sh on the next 12-24h MES outage.
items.resolution_criteria: Either IBC's nightly auto-restart reliably re-establishes the upstream IB session (verifiable by 7 consecutive days with no `Up >20h with broken farm connections` state), or a sanctioned scheduled job replaces it (e.g. systemd timer triggering vm-ib-gateway-recover at 11:55 PM UTC, before IB's reset window).
items.resolved_by: claude (/health-review — auto-heal proven on a real wedge 2026-06-03; see BL-20260528-001)
items.resolution: ROOT CAUSE REFINED 2026-05-28 (corrects the original 2FA/nightly-restart hypotheses). gateway-logs (issue #2184) showed the container 'Up 13h' but logged OUT of IBKR since `2026-05-28 04:30:50 IBC: detected dialog entitled: Unrecognized Username or Password` — i.e. during IBKR's overnight server-reset window the Gateway's in-place re-login hit a TRANSIENT bad-credential dialog and IBC parked on it, never retrying. NOT 2FA (the paper account DUQ325724 logs in with no 2FA — confirmed by the #2185 recover log: 'Click button: Paper Log In' → 'Login has completed', no Mobile prompt). NOT a real bad password (a full container restart re-logins cleanly — that's why vm-ib-gateway-recover works). NOT `restart: unless-stopped` (the process hangs on the dialog, it never dies, so Docker never restarts it). The gap: nothing auto-recovers a stuck login-failure dialog (`TWOFA_TIMEOUT_ACTION=restart` only covers 2FA timeouts, which never fire for paper). FIX (operator-approved 2026-05-28, branch claude/health-check-CjAWV, draft PR #2183): a symptom-driven auto-heal watchdog — scripts/check_ib_gateway.py + deploy/ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.{service,timer}, firing every 5 min: probes ib_paper via ib_connect_check (healthy ⇔ connected AND net_liquidation populated — a logged-out gateway reports connected=true but net_liquidation=None), and after 2 consecutive wedged checks runs scripts/ops/restart_ib_gateway.sh, with max-restarts=3 + 20-min cooldown so a genuine bad-cred/lockout can never become a restart loop (then it alert-only escalates). The fixed daily scheduled-restart option was considered and DROPPED as non-structural per operator — the watchdog self-heals any wedge within ~5-10 min, fine for delayed paper data. REMAINING: operator merge + pull-and-deploy, then verify the watchdog auto-heals across a few overnight IBKR resets (target: no manual vm-ib-gateway-recover needed).

Auto-heal watchdog shipped to draft PR #2183. 2026-05-28 (S-MES-AUTOHEAL): MERGED (#2183 -> main 6972a9f) + DEPLOYED (4d4d0c3 via pull-and-deploy #2196); watchdog confirmed LIVE — timer active + healthy probe (probe: healthy=True net_liquidation=1000600.6 action=none) in the #2196 post-restart systemd dump. items.title: FCM mobile-push notifier hits Firebase with stale / unregistered device tokens (404 NOT_FOUND)
items.description: When the trader emitted a fresh BTCUSDT VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:30Z, the M12 mobile-push notifier (src/runtime/mobile_push/notifier.py) fired FCM push attempts that came back `status=404 body={"error":{"code":404, "message":"Requested entity was not found.","status":"NOT_FOUND","details":[...FcmEr...]}}` — Google saying the device registration tokens FCM is targeting don't exist. Visible in the post-deploy journalctl on issue #2168. Means notifications aren't reaching the Android app. Either the dashboard/Android app never registered tokens after FCM init, or the tokens stored in the bot's token store are stale (device uninstall / FCM revocation / token rotation). Notifier should be marking these tokens as dead on 404 and stop retrying them; investigate whether notifier.py implements the FCM `UNREGISTERED` cleanup (remove-on-404) or silently retries. Pre-existing (the FCM key leak earlier today is unrelated — that was about the credential shape in .env, not the device tokens).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; tractable as a code-only investigation (no live-trading risk).
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) confirm + ship a token-cleanup-on-404 path in notifier.py so dead tokens are pruned automatically, or (b) confirm the cleanup already exists and the token store has been drained, and document the operator path to re-register the Android app's FCM token. The 404 cluster should stop appearing in the journal after a fresh signal emission.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: CONFIRMED STILL OCCURRING. journalctl shows a flood of 'FCM publish non-2xx: status=404 ... NOT_FOUND' on every signal emission — ~12+ per emit at 04:55:36-38 (post-boot fade signal) and again at 06:00:35-36 (vwap signal_emitted). Dead/unregistered device tokens are not being pruned on 404; the notifier keeps targeting them each emit. No token-cleanup-on-404 path is taking effect. Tier-1 code investigation in src/runtime/mobile_push/notifier.py — not editable from /health-review.

check_log.note: 2026-05-30 (/health-review, operator-confirmed): push DELIVERY IS WORKING - operator confirms notifications are landing on the phone. So the FCM 404 NOT_FOUND lines (still seen in ict-trader-live journalctl, e.g. ~23:50Z 2026-05-30) are LOG-NOISE from a stale/secondary unregistered token, NOT a delivery outage. Severity downgraded: remaining work is just pruning 404'd tokens from the registry so the notifier stops logging WARN+error bodies on every cycle. Not a user-facing problem.

items.title: VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:35Z sized to target_qty=0.0
items.description: Brand-new BTCUSDT buy signal emitted at 17:57:35Z (entry 74790.9, deviation -1.36σ below VWAP, regime=weak-down/medium, policy_allow=True). Aggregated with fade_breakout_4h in same-direction reinforcement (`aggregation_reason: same_direction_reinforcement: 2 intents agree on long; max target_qty=0.0 from strategy=vwap`). But `target_qty=0.0` from the intent layer means the aggregated package size is zero — coordinator would either skip dispatch or refuse via the zero-qty gate. Could be a transient balance/risk-cap condition (daily_loss_budget exhausted, available USDT below min_balance, sizing math underflow), or a config drift. One observation, not yet a pattern. Next /performance-review window should grade the post-deploy VWAP cohort and check whether target_qty stays at zero (= effective shadow even though execution=live) or recovers.

items.triage_note: 2026-05-28 /health-review: NOT transient — sustained on every in-window vwap signal. Two distinct contributors now visible: (1) vwap is execution:shadow, so the intent layer emits target_qty=0.0 BY DESIGN (journalctl 06:00: "vwap emitted intent side=long target_qty=0.000000 priority=40"); (2) on bybit_1 (demo) the trades table shows status=rejected risk_refused sized_qty=0 'check daily-loss budget...' — bybit_1 closed -108 on 2026-05-28 (trade 1784) which exceeds its daily_usd=100 cap, so RiskManager is correctly refusing further trades for the UTC day (Prime-Directive per-trade refusal, not a bug). items.source: Today's FCM-credential bleed incident (issue #2157).
items.title: Operator-only follow-ups from the FCM private-key bleed
items.description:
The 2026-05-27 systemd-EnvironmentFile bleed dumped the FCM service-account PEM private key into the journalctl tail on TWO pull-and-deploy issue comments. PR #2158 shipped the scrub-env-noncompliant system-action and it ran successfully (12 lines stripped, journal warning is now gone — verified on issue #2161). What's left is operator-only because the MCP tools I have can neither delete issue comments (no delete_issue_comment) nor vacuum the journal (no journalctl vacuum system-action) nor rotate the FCM key (that's Google Cloud Console). Three steps:
  1. Delete the comments containing the leaked key (one-click `...` → Delete on each):
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2157#issuecomment-4555473441
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2159#issuecomment-4555811974
  2. Rotate the FCM service-account key at Google Cloud Console (only step that actually neutralizes the exposure — the journal copies on the VM, webhook archives, and any third-party GitHub mirror still hold the old key). After rotation, re-dispatch `set-mobile-push-secrets` with the new JSON in the FCM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON secret to update the on-disk file at ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json.
  3. Optional: `sudo journalctl --rotate && sudo journalctl --vacuum-time=1s` on the live VM to wipe historical journal copies containing the key. Reduces forensic surface; the scrub already stops future bleeds at source.
items.trigger_condition: Operator-only — no autonomous action possible. Surface every /health-review run until resolved.
items.resolution_criteria: Both leaked issue comments deleted (404 on the comment URLs); FCM service-account key rotated at Google Cloud Console; ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json updated to the new credential and a fresh push test succeeds (kicks the notifier and confirms no 404 NOT_FOUND).

items.source: Operator request at session close — confirm the deployed IB Gateway watchdog actually heals a real wedge, not just that it probes healthy.
items.title: Verify the ict-ib-gateway-watchdog RESTART path fires end-to-end on a real overnight IBKR-reset wedge
items.description: The auto-heal watchdog (the BL-20260527-003 fix, PR #2183) went live on the trader VM on 2026-05-28 (main 6972a9f, deployed as 4d4d0c3 via pull-and-deploy #2196). Its HEALTHY path is confirmed — the #2196 post-restart systemd dump shows `probe: healthy=True reason=net_liquidation=1000600.6 action=none` and the timer active (TriggeredBy). What is NOT yet observed in production is the RESTART path: that on a genuine wedge (a logged-out Gateway reports `connected=true` but `net_liquidation=None`) the watchdog actually runs `scripts/ops/restart_ib_gateway.sh` and brings `ib_paper`/MES back with no human in the loop. The natural test is IBKR's overnight server-reset window (~04:30 UTC), the same window that produced the 2026-05-28 wedge. The watchdog journal is now readable from the diag surface — `ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.{service,timer}` was added to diag.py `_CANONICAL_UNITS` in #2192, so `/api/diag/journalctl?unit=ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.service` works.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run on or after 2026-05-29: pull `/api/diag/journalctl?unit=ict-ib-gateway-watchdog.service` covering the overnight window (use ?since=/?until= for the ~04:00-05:30 UTC reset) and read the per-fire `probe:` lines.
items.resolution_criteria: A /health-review confirms in the watchdog journal at least one real wedge that the watchdog auto-recovered: a probe `healthy=False` (net_liquidation=None) followed within ~2 checks by a logged `action=restart`, then a subsequent probe `healthy=True` — with NO manual vm-ib-gateway-recover dispatch in that window. Confirm across at least one (ideally a couple) overnight resets, then mark BL-20260527-003 fully resolved. If MES instead stayed healthy through the reset (no wedge to heal), record that and keep watching — absence of a wedge is not yet proof the restart path works. If guard rails tripped (`max-restarts 3` exhausted -> alert-only), surface to the operator: that means a genuine lockout the watchdog can't fix.
items.resolution: RESTART PATH VERIFIED END-TO-END on a genuine overnight IBKR-reset wedge (2026-06-03). ict-ib-gateway-watchdog journal (diag issues #2662/#2664): 04:17+04:22 probe healthy=True; 04:27:40 probe healthy=False (TimeoutError, gateway unreachable) action=detected; 04:32:41 probe healthy=False action=restart -> `sudo docker restart ib-gateway` (restart attempt 1/3); 04:34:09 `[ACTION] IB Gateway auto-healed — restarted ib-gateway; IBC login completed`; 04:37:27 probe healthy=True net_liquidation=1001115.4 action=recovered `[OK] IB Gateway recovered. ib_paper reconnected; MES data flowing again.`; sustained healthy=True action=none every ~5 min 04:42->05:28. NO manual vm-ib-gateway-recover dispatch. Matches the resolution criteria (healthy=False -> action=restart within 2 checks -> subsequent healthy=True, no human). The guard rails were not exhausted (1/3). Closing this AND BL-20260527-003 (the IBC-nightly-restart root issue it gates) — the symptom-driven auto-heal is now proven on a real wedge. NOTE: a SEPARATE consequence of this same wedge is logged as BL-20260603-001 (the trader's main-thread IB-socket blocking stalled the heartbeat and tripped the liveness-watchdog autoheal).

items.source: S-ANDROID-S9 session (diag pulls while debugging the Android app)
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay rejects multi-param diag paths (the & query separator)
items.description: Issue-driven diag requests whose title carries a & failed this session (#2251 `journal?table=order_packages&limit=40`, #2252 `journal?table=trades&limit=40`) while single-param paths (`journal?table=order_packages`, `db_info`) and the broad `snapshot?limit=200` succeeded. Suspected cause: GitHub stores the title's & as the HTML entity &; the workflow decodes it (${raw//&/&}) but the path-validation regex ^[A-Za-z0-9/?&=_.:%-]+$ in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml rejects the ; if any entity slips through un-decoded, so the run exits 1 with 'Rejected diag_path'. Workaround in use: single-param paths or the broad snapshot. Low priority — the relay works for the common cases.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run, or the next time a session needs a multi-param diag pull.
items.resolution_criteria: A multi-param diag request (e.g. journal?table=trades&limit=40) completes and posts JSON back, OR the workflow title decode/validation is fixed (fully decode & before the regex, or URL-decode) and verified with a multi-param pull.
items.resolution: VERIFIED FIXED EMPIRICALLY 2026-05-29: a fresh multi-param pull `journal?table=trades&limit=5` (issue #2315) returned JSON (5 rows) and auto-closed — the resolution criteria's first clause is met. vm-diag-snapshot.yml already carries the `${raw//&/&}` entity-decode (lines ~123) and the path-validation regex already allows `&` (`^[A-Za-z0-9/?&=_.:%-]+$`), so the multi-param path now decodes and validates correctly. The prior-session failures (#2251/#2252) were not reproduced; no code change required. NOTE: the EARLIER multi-param failures observed in THIS session's first half (#2293/#2294 etc.) were the ict-web-api deploy-bounce outage (~09:45-09:52Z), not the & decode — single-param paths failed in that same window too.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-29 — a docs-only commit deploy bounced the live trader + web-api mid-review, and the (unnecessary) recover dispatches were all webhook-dropped.
items.title: git-sync deploy restarts the LIVE TRADER for docs-only commits (brief trading + read-surface interruption); web-api has no VM-side self-heal and the GitHub recover dispatch is webhook-flaky
items.description: OBSERVED this review: between status #2286 (09:44:05Z, uptime 36577s, git_sha b149b15) and status #2302 (09:52:15Z, uptime 405s, git_sha 22e2cf0) the live trader RESTARTED and HEAD advanced b149b15 -> 22e2cf0. The only commit in that range is 22e2cf0 (#2284, a DOCS-ONLY 'docs(health-review): recurring carry-forward item' change). So the ict-git-sync timer pulled the new commit and ran the full deploy_pull_restart path, which restarts ALL ict-* units (trader + web-api) regardless of whether the diff touches any runtime code. Two consequences: (1) the live money-path trader was bounced (~7 min, 09:45-09:52Z) for a pure documentation change — an unnecessary trading interruption; (2) during that window ict-web-api was down so the diag/dashboard READ surface was blinded (diag pulls #2293-#2296,#2298 failed 09:45:44-09:48:03Z). I misread (2) as a crash and fired three vm-web-api-recover dispatches (#2297/#2299/#2300) — ALL webhook-dropped (issue OPEN, updated_at==created_at, no run), same GitHub issues.opened batching as the resolved BL-20260527-001. They turned out UNNECESSARY (the deploy completed and both services self-recovered by 09:52Z). Net: no real outage, but two genuine gaps surfaced. PRIMARY GAP: deploy_pull_restart restarts the live trader for non-runtime (docs/comms) commits — consider gating the trader restart on whether the diff touches runtime paths (src/, config/, deploy/, requirements). SECONDARY GAP: ict-web-api has no VM-side self-heal and the only autonomous recover lever (GitHub-dispatched vm-web-api-recover) is webhook-flaky exactly when needed.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever a deploy of a docs/comms-only commit is observed to bounce the live trader, or a vm-web-api-recover dispatch is webhook-dropped.
items.resolution_criteria: PRIMARY: deploy_pull_restart.sh (or the git-sync path) skips the ict-trader-live restart when the pulled diff touches no runtime path (docs/comms-only) — verify a docs-only commit deploys without a trader uptime reset. SECONDARY: ict-web-api gets a VM-side auto-restart-on-unhealthy guard (systemd watchdog/timer probing local :8001 /api/health) so the read surface self-heals without a webhook-dependent dispatch. Both are Tier-2 deploy/runtime changes; not editable from /health-review — surface to the operator.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-29T19:40Z /health-review: RECURRED AGAIN this review, both gaps. (1) A docs/comms-only commit (d4e4a0c, HEAD at 19:28:42Z) was pulled by ict-git-sync and ran the full deploy_pull_restart — status #2375 shows the live trader bounced to bot_uptime_s=31 at 19:35:46Z (git_sha d4e4a0c) and web-api was down 19:29-19:35Z. The first 7 diag pulls of this review (#2367-2371) failed during that window and 4 rapid re-fires (#2378-2381) webhook-dropped (BL-20260527-001 batching). (2) vm-web-api-recover #2373 FAILED (wrapper exit 1) — it ran mid-bounce: restart_web_api.sh saw ict-web-api stop-sigterm time out (SIGKILL at 19:32:57) then a slow uvicorn boot, so its /api/health probe got curl-7 connection-refused for ~60s and gave up; the service self-recovered by 19:35. Net: ~6 min read-surface blind + 1 unnecessary failed recover, no real trading outage, all self-healed. This is now the 2nd consecutive review (cf. the in-description 09:45Z event) where a non-runtime commit bounced the money-path trader — the PRIMARY gap (gate trader restart on whether the diff touches runtime paths) is worth prioritising with the operator. | 2026-06-01 /health-review: REPRODUCED A 3RD TIME. status pull (issue #2585, captured 16:57:17Z) shows bot_uptime_s=341 (~5.7 min → restart ~16:51:36Z) on git_sha=f91aa32, which is the 2026-06-01 'night session-end' DOCS-ONLY commit (#2584). So a documentation-only commit again ran the full deploy_pull_restart and bounced the live trader. The trader recovered healthy (heartbeat 'running' age 107s, last_tick 16:56:58Z, all 9 strategies loaded, no crashloop). Persistent low-grade trading/read-surface interruption on every docs commit; PRIMARY gap (gate the restart on runtime-path diffs) still the priority Tier-2 fix for the operator.

The 22:29Z stop coincides almost exactly with the last live trade id=1866 @ 2026-05-28 22:25:01 and the start of the ~11.5h no-signal lull that BL-20260529-001 is already tracking -- so the regime-shadow feed likely shares a root cause with that lull (regime predictors invoked on a market-bar / signal-eval path that went quiet), NOT the 09:45-09:52Z git-sync restart (which is later). RESOLVED - root cause is code-level + benign, confirming the 19:40Z suspicion. Shadow predictions are emitted ONLY as a side-effect of an actionable buy/sell signal (src/runtime/strategy_signal_builders.py::_emit_shadow_preds), not per-tick. Live audit relay 2026-05-30T07:52-08:05Z: ALL 6 strategies x {BTCUSDT,MES} eval to side=none every ~75s (vwap regime_policy_skip 'regime=sideways/low'; donchian in-channel; fade ADX 34-35 not chop; squeeze no release; turtle/ict_scalp no sweep), zero exceptions, trader healthy (uptime ~11h, last_tick 07:43Z). So every multi-hour shadow 'stall' = a no-signal lull (quiet sideways market, vwap regime-gated), NOT a wiring break. DOCUMENTED ANSWER to the higher-TF question: feature_row_for_predictor (src/runtime/regime_shadow.py) returns None for a regime model whose (symbol,timeframe) does not match the firing signal's, so on a ~5m vwap/ict_scalp signal ONLY btc-regime-5m is scored - the 15m/1h/MES regime heads are skipped BY DESIGN and cannot log via the signal-gated path. Resolution_criteria met (by-design note that only the timeframe-matching regime feeds shadow). Forward-looking coverage improvement (a per-bar regime-scoring path) tracked in ml-review-backlog MB-20260529-001.

items.source: Reading insights_history (relay-reachable via BL-20260529-004) — the M13 vwap strategy insight's prose contradicts its own structured row_counts.
items.title: INVESTIGATE: M13 template-analyst vwap strategy insight self-contradicts — summary_md says 'Trades 89/0 closed, WR n/a (0W/0L)' while the same row's row_counts says trades_closed=10, wins=1, losses=9
items.description: insights_history row id=1469 (endpoint=strategy, strategy_name=vwap, model_id=template:v1, generated_at 2026-05-29T20:35:17Z): summary_md renders 'Trades (total / closed): 89 / 0 ... Win rate: n/a (0W / 0L)', but the SAME row's row_counts_json is {trades:89, trades_closed:10, wins:1, losses:9, order_packages:20} and net PnL is rendered -$2.98 (which is non-zero, implying closed trades exist). So the markdown 'closed=0 / 0W-0L' disagrees with the structured 'closed=10 / 1W-9L / -$2.98'. The goal is NOT just to confirm the discrepancy but to ROOT-CAUSE it in the template analyst (src/runtime/insights/template_analyst.py + generator.py): likely two different closed-trade definitions feeding the same card — e.g. the prose 'closed' counts rows with a terminal close timestamp while row_counts 'trades_closed' counts a different status, OR a shadow-vs-live filter applied to one path but not the other (vwap is execution:shadow, 89 trades / mostly unfilled), OR a window/symbol-join mismatch. Whichever it is, the two numbers should be reconciled so the operator-facing prose matches the structured counts. Cosmetic-but-misleading: a reader sees 'vwap: 0 closed, WR n/a' in the narrative while the data says 1W/9L -$2.98.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/runtime/insights/. Re-pull insights_history (endpoint=strategy, vwap) and diff summary_md's closed/WR against row_counts to confirm it still skews; then trace the two closed-trade computations in template_analyst.py.

items.source: Holistic exit-execution audit triggered by trend_donchian's 0%-win record (issues #2542-#2547).
items.title: High order_package ORPHAN rates across strategies — positions go DB-open / exchange-flat without a clean fill record
items.description: While auditing whether strategies exit properly, found high orphan rates in order_packages: trend_donchian 22/38 (58%), vwap 917/1460 (63%), squeeze 24/83 (29%), ict_scalp 4/13 (31%). 'Orphaned' = the reconciler found the DB-open package flat on the exchange but could not record a clean fill/PnL (vs reconciler_filled where it could). This loses PnL attribution and inflates the 'rejected'/'orphaned' noise in the journal, which can skew per-strategy performance stats and therefore the research/demotion evidence. Likely contributors: (a) the re-entry storm (fixed separately by the bar-close debounce) creating packages that never stabilise into real positions; (b) entry orders that fill-then-immediately-exchange-SL vs orders that never fill (the 341 exchange_rejected + intent_noop rejections suggest a mix); (c) the reconciler/orphan-handler not querying Bybit get_closed_pnl to attribute the realised PnL on a derivatives SL/TP fire (see order_monitor._classify_orphan_close — derivatives orphans are left 'unknown'). NOTE the exit machinery itself works (fade + ict_scalp win normally through reconciler_filled; the Chandelier trail ratchets) — this is a reconciliation/attribution + order-stabilisation issue, not a broken-exit issue.
items.trigger_condition: After the bar-debounce deploys (removes the re-entry-storm contributor), re-measure orphan rates; then root-cause the residual orphans with a live order-placement + Bybit get_closed_pnl trace for one fresh derivatives dispatch.
items.resolution_criteria: Orphan rate materially reduced and/or derivatives orphans get their realised PnL attributed (reconciler_filled with pnl) instead of orphaned-with-null-pnl. Per-strategy stats stop being polluted by phantom/orphan rows.
items.triage_note: 2026-06-01 /health-review: re-measured post bar-debounce (the audit tail now carries bar_debounce_blocked events, so the re-entry-storm contributor is deployed). order_packages pull (issue #2593, limit=40, 20 salvaged before the 55KB truncation): 18/20 recent packages still ORPHANED — but ALL are SHADOW strategies (16 vwap + 2 htf_pullback_trend_2h), i.e. data-only logging clutter, NOT a real-money position stranded. SL+TP present on all 20. The LIVE-path orphan concern (a real position going DB-open/exchange-flat with null pnl) was NOT exercised this quiet window — turtle_soup/trend_donchian(LONG-ONLY)/ict_scalp_5m all eval'd side=none, no live dispatch. So the residual orphans are dominated by shadow vwap (consistent with the 917/1460≈63% vwap rate in the original audit); the derivatives-PnL-attribution root-cause (order_monitor._classify_orphan_close) remains the open Tier-2 dev item. Graded health-review 'orders: watch'.

items.source: Noticed while investigating the MES (mes_trend_long_1d) IBKR-paper pipeline (PR #2626): order_packages + signal-audit pulls show htf_pullback_trend_2h producing MES order packages even though its config declares symbols:[BTCUSDT] and it routes only to bybit_1.
items.title: Per-strategy `symbols:` does not gate EVALUATION — the multi-symbol tick loop runs every enabled builder on the union of all account symbols, producing cross-product packages no account routes (ORPHANED)
items.description: The tick loop (src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols) unions every configured account's symbols ({BTCUSDT, MES}) and evaluates each enabled strategy's signal builder on every symbol in the union, regardless of the strategy's own `symbols:` field in config/strategies.yaml. Observed 2026-06-02: htf_pullback_trend_2h (symbols:[BTCUSDT], routed to bybit_1 only) emitted MES order packages (status 'orphaned'); conversely mes_trend_long_1d (symbols:[MES]) is evaluated on BTCUSDT (the signal audit shows symbol=BTCUSDT eval rows for it, reason 'short_suppressed_long_only'). These cross-product packages are dropped/orphaned because no account routes that (strategy x symbol) combo — the symbol->exchange dispatch gate (coordinator._eligible_for_dispatch) + the account.strategies filter correctly prevent execution, so this is HARMLESS to trading (no wrong-symbol order can fire). But it is wasted per-tick evaluation + journal noise: the orphaned cross-product rows pollute per-strategy orphan-rate stats (cf. BL-20260601-001, whose triage_note already saw these htf_pullback_trend_2h-on-MES orphans). Fix: gate EVALUATION by the strategy's own declared `symbols:` (intersected with its routed accounts' symbols) so a BTCUSDT-only strategy is never run on MES and vice-versa.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review or any session touching src/main.py::_resolve_tick_symbols or the per-tick strategy-evaluation loop. Re-pull order_packages and confirm orphaned cross-product packages (htf_pullback_trend_2h on MES, mes_trend_long_1d on BTCUSDT) still appear.
items.resolution_criteria: Strategy signal builders are evaluated only on the symbols the strategy declares in config/strategies.yaml (intersected with account routing), so no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) order packages are produced. Verify htf_pullback_trend_2h no longer emits MES packages and mes_trend_long_1d no longer emits BTCUSDT packages, with no loss of legitimate signals.
items.resolution_note: Fixed by the per-strategy symbol-scope gate (PR #2643, merged + deployed 2026-06-02, HEAD 3d35e24). intent_multiplexer._collect_intents now skips a strategy whose declared config/strategies.yaml symbols: do not include the current tick symbol (permissive on no-symbols/unknown/config-error), so a strategy is evaluated/emits ONLY on its declared instrument(s) — no cross-product (strategy x non-declared-symbol) packages are produced. Gated at intent EMISSION (not dispatch) so a higher-priority off-symbol strategy can't win a symbol's per-symbol aggregation and starve the legitimate owner. Verified live post-deploy via audit_query: mgc_pullback_1d evaluates MGC-only (zero MES/MHG/BTCUSDT rows) where pre-deploy it ran on all four. Crypto strategies (symbols:[BTCUSDT]) no longer evaluate on MES. Tests: tests/test_intent_symbol_scope.py (4) + 324 intent/coordinator/pipeline regression green. NOTE: the gate lives in the production intent-multiplexer path (STRATEGY=multiplexed_intents); the legacy first-wins pipeline path is not gated but is not the production path.
